notes: Gold consumable rules for two tables: onet_work_profiles (798 rows, grain=bls_soc_code) and career_transitions (15,944 rows, grain=bls_soc_code x related_bls_soc_code). HMN score uses min/max rescaling to fill 1-10 range (design change post-EDA). Burnout score range 3.48-8.32 from EDA, validated against 1-10 domain bounds. 24 partial-data occupations produce null scores. Golden dataset rule DEFERRED pending golden dataset creation by @primary-agent. Concept normalization patterns (CONS-COLLISION-RESOLVED, CONS-COVERAGE-FLOOR) do not apply -- single-source spec with no concept normalization.

rules.name: Work profiles grain uniqueness: bls_soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT bls_soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles GROUP BY bls_soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per bls_soc_code. Any duplicate indicates a promote or transformer failure. Maps to patterns CONS-GRAIN-UNIQUE and ADV-GRAIN-UNIQUE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 798 distinct bls_soc_code values across 798 rows in base.onet_occupations (100% unique). Logical model declares bls_soc_code as NK with zero-duplicate constraint. Threshold: 100% unique.

rules.name: Work profiles record_id uniqueness and not null
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'NULL_CHECK' AS record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE record_id IS NULL HAVING cnt > 0
rules.description: Surrogate key record_id (computed via compute_grain_id with prefix 'wp') must be non-null and unique.
rules.rationale: Logical model: record_id is PK, NOT NULL. Deterministic SHA-256 hash of natural key (bls_soc_code) should produce 0 collisions for 798 rows.

rules.name: Work profiles row count exactly 798
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 798 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles
rules.description: Gold table must contain exactly 798 rows -- one per occupation from base.onet_occupations. All Silver rows carried forward, no filtering.
rules.rationale: EDA report: base.onet_occupations has exactly 798 rows. Spec: 'Row count: 798 (one per occupation).' This is a 1:1 carry-forward from the Silver occupation table.

rules.name: SOC code format: XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE bls_soc_code IS NULL OR bls_soc_code !~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$'
rules.description: Every bls_soc_code must match SOC standard format XX-XXXX (7 characters). This is the primary join key to consumable.occupation_profiles.
rules.rationale: EDA report: all 798 bls_soc_code values match XX-XXXX format, 7 characters, 0 nulls. Silver DQ validates this upstream.

rules.name: HMN score range: 1.0-10.0 for non-null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE hmn_score IS NOT NULL AND (hmn_score < 1.0 OR hmn_score > 10.0)
rules.description: hmn_score must be between 1.0 and 10.0 inclusive for all non-null rows. Values outside this range indicate a rescaling formula error. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA pre-rescale range was 3.46-4.94. Post-rescale design change uses min/max normalization to fill 1.0-10.0 range, clamped at boundaries. Domain constraint: 1-10 scale. All scored occupations (774) must fall within bounds.

rules.name: HMN score null count exactly 24
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 24 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE hmn_score IS NULL
rules.description: Exactly 24 partial-data occupations should have null hmn_score. More nulls indicate a join failure; fewer indicate incorrect scoring of partial-data occupations.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 24 occupations with data_completeness_tier='partial' have neither activity nor context profiles. These 24 must have null HMN scores. 774 scored occupations confirmed.

rules.name: Burnout score range: 1.0-10.0 for non-null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE burnout_score IS NOT NULL AND (burnout_score < 1.0 OR burnout_score > 10.0)
rules.description: burnout_score must be between 1.0 and 10.0 inclusive for all non-null rows. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: simulated burnout scores range 3.48-8.32, all within 1.0-10.0. Formula maps normalized average to 1+9*avg, mathematically bounded [1.0, 10.0]. Domain constraint: 1-10 scale.

rules.name: Burnout score null count exactly 24
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 24 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE burnout_score IS NULL
rules.description: Exactly 24 partial-data occupations should have null burnout_score. Same set as null hmn_score.
rules.rationale: EDA report: the 24 partial-data occupations lack both activity AND context profiles (perfectly correlated). burnout_score derives from context profiles, so the same 24 must be null.

rules.name: HMN and burnout scores null in same rows
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE (hmn_score IS NULL AND burnout_score IS NOT NULL) OR (hmn_score IS NOT NULL AND burnout_score IS NULL)
rules.description: hmn_score and burnout_score must be null in exactly the same rows. A mismatch would indicate a partial join bug. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: all 24 partial-data occupations lack BOTH activity and context profiles (perfectly correlated). No occupation has one profile type but not the other. Therefore hmn_score and burnout_score must be null or non-null together.

rules.name: HMN score meaningful spread (std dev > 1.0)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN STDDEV_SAMP(hmn_score) > 1.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE hmn_score IS NOT NULL
rules.description: After min/max rescaling, hmn_score should have standard deviation > 1.0, indicating meaningful differentiation across occupations. Maps to ADV-DISTRIBUTION-VARIANCE pattern.
rules.rationale: Pre-rescale EDA showed std=0.212 (compressed into 1.5 points). The design change to min/max rescaling will spread values across the full 1-10 range. Post-rescale std should be well above 1.0 (expected ~2.0-3.0). Threshold of 1.0 is conservative floor.

rules.name: Burnout score spread (std dev > 0.5)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN STDDEV_SAMP(burnout_score) > 0.5 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE burnout_score IS NOT NULL
rules.description: Burnout score should have standard deviation > 0.5, indicating meaningful differentiation. Maps to ADV-DISTRIBUTION-VARIANCE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: burnout_score std=0.778, range 3.48-8.32 (4.8 points of spread). Already healthy distribution. Threshold of 0.5 provides safety margin below observed 0.778.

rules.name: hmn_score_rounded matches hmn_score
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE hmn_score IS NOT NULL AND (hmn_score_rounded IS NULL OR hmn_score_rounded != ROUND(hmn_score)::INT) UNION ALL SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE hmn_score IS NULL AND hmn_score_rounded IS NOT NULL
rules.description: hmn_score_rounded must equal ROUND(hmn_score) when hmn_score is non-null, and be null when hmn_score is null. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: Spec: 'hmn_score_rounded = ROUND(hmn_score)'. Derived column must be consistent with source column. Integer range 1-10 for pentagon display.

rules.name: burnout_score_rounded matches burnout_score
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE burnout_score IS NOT NULL AND (burnout_score_rounded IS NULL OR burnout_score_rounded != ROUND(burnout_score)::INT) UNION ALL SELECT COUNT(*) FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE burnout_score IS NULL AND burnout_score_rounded IS NOT NULL
rules.description: burnout_score_rounded must equal ROUND(burnout_score) when burnout_score is non-null, and be null when burnout_score is null.
rules.rationale: Spec: 'burnout_score_rounded = ROUND(burnout_score)'. Same derivation consistency rule as GLD-ONP-012.

rules.name: top_human_activities is valid JSON array when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE hmn_score IS NOT NULL AND (top_human_activities IS NULL OR TRY_CAST(top_human_activities AS JSON) IS NULL OR json_array_length(top_human_activities::JSON) < 1)
rules.description: top_human_activities must be a valid JSON array with at least 1 element for all scored occupations. Null allowed only when hmn_score is null.
rules.rationale: Spec: 'Top 5 work activities by importance that are classified as human-intensive.' All 774 scored occupations have all 41 activities including the 14 human-intensive ones, so the JSON array will always have content.

rules.name: burnout_drivers is valid JSON array when non-null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE burnout_score IS NOT NULL AND (burnout_drivers IS NULL OR TRY_CAST(burnout_drivers AS JSON) IS NULL OR json_array_length(burnout_drivers::JSON) < 1)
rules.description: burnout_drivers must be a valid JSON array with at least 1 element for all scored occupations. Null allowed only when burnout_score is null.
rules.rationale: Spec: 'Top 3 burnout-contributing Work Context elements.' All 774 scored occupations have all 9 burnout elements, so the array will always have at least 3 entries.

rules.name: time_pressure range 1-5
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE time_pressure IS NOT NULL AND (time_pressure < 1.0 OR time_pressure > 5.0)
rules.description: time_pressure (element 4.C.3.d.1) is a CX scale value with range 1-5. Maps to ADV-VALUE-RANGE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: Time Pressure mean=3.847, range 1.67-5.00, scale CX (1-5). Domain constraint: CX scale is 1-5.

rules.name: work_hours range 1-3
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE work_hours IS NOT NULL AND (work_hours < 1.0 OR work_hours > 3.0)
rules.description: work_hours (element 4.C.3.d.8) is a CT scale value with range 1-3 (where 3 = >40 hrs). Maps to ADV-VALUE-RANGE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: Duration of Typical Work Week mean=2.273, range 1.00-3.00, scale CT (1-3). Domain constraint: CT scale is 1-3.

rules.name: consequence_of_error range 1-5
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE consequence_of_error IS NOT NULL AND (consequence_of_error < 1.0 OR consequence_of_error > 5.0)
rules.description: consequence_of_error (element 4.C.3.a.1) is a CX scale value with range 1-5. Maps to ADV-VALUE-RANGE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: Consequence of Error mean=3.015, range 1.33-4.97, scale CX (1-5). Domain constraint: CX scale is 1-5.

rules.name: Confidence tier distribution: 772 high, 2 medium, 24 low
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN high_ct = 772 AND med_ct = 2 AND low_ct = 24 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE confidence_tier = 'high') AS high_ct, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE confidence_tier = 'medium') AS med_ct, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE confidence_tier = 'low') AS low_ct FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles)
rules.description: Confidence tier distribution must match expected counts: 772 high, 2 medium (SOC 29-1241 Ophthalmologists with 10.5% context suppression + SOC 51-2061 edge case with >=5% suppression), 24 low (partial-data occupations).
rules.rationale: EDA predicted 773/1/24 but actual execution found 772/2/24. SOC 51-2061 was an edge case that crossed the 5% suppression threshold, moving from high to medium. Verified against warehouse data post-implementation.

rules.name: confidence_tier values are valid enum
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE confidence_tier NOT IN ('high', 'medium', 'low')
rules.description: confidence_tier must be one of: high, medium, low. No nulls allowed (required field per spec).
rules.rationale: Spec defines exactly three tiers with explicit derivation rules. Logical model: confidence_tier is required (NOT NULL).

rules.name: suppress_pct_activities within valid range
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE suppress_pct_activities IS NOT NULL AND (suppress_pct_activities < 0.0 OR suppress_pct_activities > 100.0)
rules.description: Activity suppression percentage must be between 0 and 100. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: only 1 suppressed activity row out of 31,734 (0.003%). Max per-SOC suppression is well below 5%. Percentage cannot exceed 100 or be negative by definition.

rules.name: suppress_pct_context within valid range
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE suppress_pct_context IS NOT NULL AND (suppress_pct_context < 0.0 OR suppress_pct_context > 100.0)
rules.description: Context suppression percentage must be between 0 and 100.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 18 suppressed context rows out of 44,118 (0.04%). Max per-SOC is 10.5% (SOC 29-1241). All within 0-100 bounds.

rules.name: Required identity fields not null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE bls_soc_code IS NULL OR primary_title IS NULL OR description IS NULL OR data_completeness_tier IS NULL OR confidence_tier IS NULL OR backs_stats IS NULL OR backs_bosses IS NULL OR source_load_date IS NULL OR promoted_at IS NULL
rules.description: All required identity, quality, and metadata fields must be non-null for every row.
rules.rationale: Spec marks these fields as 'required: yes'. EDA confirms 0% null rate for bls_soc_code, primary_title, description in source. Metadata fields are generated at promote time.

rules.name: backs_stats = 'HMN' for all rows
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE backs_stats != 'HMN'
rules.description: backs_stats must be 'HMN' for all rows (static field per spec).
rules.rationale: Spec: 'backs_stats: HMN for all rows.' This is a static assignment, not data-derived.

rules.name: backs_bosses = 'AI,Burnout' for all rows
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE backs_bosses != 'AI,Burnout'
rules.description: backs_bosses must be 'AI,Burnout' for all rows (static field per spec).
rules.rationale: Spec: 'backs_bosses: AI,Burnout for all rows.' Static assignment.

rules.name: data_completeness_tier distribution: 774 full, 24 partial
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN full_ct = 774 AND partial_ct = 24 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE data_completeness_tier = 'full') AS full_ct, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE data_completeness_tier = 'partial') AS partial_ct FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles)
rules.description: data_completeness_tier must have exactly 774 'full' and 24 'partial' rows, carried from Silver.
rules.rationale: EDA report: data_completeness_tier distribution in base.onet_occupations is 'full'=774 (97.0%), 'partial'=24 (3.0%). Carried forward 1:1.

rules.name: activity_profile_available consistent with data_completeness_tier
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE (data_completeness_tier = 'full' AND activity_profile_available != true) OR (data_completeness_tier = 'partial' AND activity_profile_available != false)
rules.description: activity_profile_available must be true for 'full' occupations and false for 'partial' ones. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: has_work_activities distribution perfectly correlates with data_completeness_tier -- all 774 full have activities, all 24 partial do not.

rules.name: top_5_activities is valid JSON array for scored occupations
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE activity_profile_available = true AND (top_5_activities IS NULL OR TRY_CAST(top_5_activities AS JSON) IS NULL OR json_array_length(top_5_activities::JSON) != 5)
rules.description: top_5_activities must be a valid JSON array with exactly 5 elements for all occupations with activity profiles.
rules.rationale: Spec: 'Top 5 activities by importance rank.' Each of the 774 scored occupations has 41 activity rows, so top-5 selection always produces exactly 5.

rules.name: Career transitions grain uniqueness: bls_soc_code x related_bls_soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT bls_soc_code, related_bls_soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_transitions GROUP BY bls_soc_code, related_bls_soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per bls_soc_code x related_bls_soc_code. Maps to CONS-GRAIN-UNIQUE and ADV-GRAIN-UNIQUE patterns.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Grain uniqueness: 0 duplicates (bls_soc_code x related_bls_soc_code is unique).' 15,944 rows with 0 duplicate grain combinations in Silver source.

rules.name: Career transitions record_id uniqueness and not null
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_transitions GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'NULL_CHECK' AS record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_transitions WHERE record_id IS NULL HAVING cnt > 0
rules.description: Surrogate key record_id (computed via compute_grain_id with prefix 'tr') must be non-null and unique.
rules.rationale: Logical model: record_id is PK, NOT NULL. Deterministic SHA-256 hash of composite key (bls_soc_code, related_bls_soc_code) should produce 0 collisions for 15,944 rows.

rules.name: Career transitions row count exactly 15944
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 15944 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_transitions
rules.description: Gold table must contain exactly 15,944 rows -- carried 1:1 from base.onet_career_transitions with title enrichment, no filtering.
rules.rationale: EDA report: base.onet_career_transitions has exactly 15,944 rows. Spec: '15,944 (carried from Silver, enriched with titles and flags).'

rules.name: No self-references in career transitions
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_transitions WHERE bls_soc_code = related_bls_soc_code
rules.description: No occupation should transition to itself. A self-reference indicates a data error in the similarity graph. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Self-references: 0 (confirmed).' This is a domain constraint -- a career transition to yourself is meaningless.

rules.name: All transition titles non-null (join success)
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_transitions WHERE source_title IS NULL OR related_title IS NULL
rules.description: source_title and related_title must be non-null for all rows. A null title indicates a failed join to base.onet_occupations. Maps to ADV-FK-VALID pattern (join completeness).
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Referential integrity: All source and related SOCs exist in base.onet_occupations.' All 798 source and 796 related SOCs have occupations with non-null primary_title. 0 orphan joins expected.

rules.name: backs_feature = 'Stage3Branching' for all rows
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_transitions WHERE backs_feature != 'Stage3Branching'
rules.description: backs_feature must be 'Stage3Branching' for all rows (static field per spec).
rules.rationale: Spec: 'backs_feature: Stage3Branching for all rows.' Static assignment.

rules.name: relatedness_tier valid enum values
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_transitions WHERE relatedness_tier NOT IN ('Primary-Short', 'Primary-Long', 'Supplemental')
rules.description: relatedness_tier must be one of: Primary-Short, Primary-Long, Supplemental.
rules.rationale: EDA report: relatedness_tier distribution -- Primary-Short=4,134 (25.9%), Primary-Long=3,938 (24.7%), Supplemental=7,872 (49.4%). No other values observed. Spec defines these three tiers based on best_index ranges.

rules.name: relationship_type = 'similarity' for all rows
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_transitions WHERE relationship_type != 'similarity'
rules.description: relationship_type must be 'similarity' for all rows. Current O*NET data only includes similarity-based transitions.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'relationship_type: similarity = 15,944 (100%).' All rows in Silver source have this value.

rules.name: best_index range 1-20
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_transitions WHERE best_index < 1 OR best_index > 20
rules.description: best_index (similarity rank) must be between 1 and 20 inclusive. Maps to ADV-VALUE-RANGE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'best_index range: 1 to 20.' Distribution roughly uniform at 780-860 rows per index value. O*NET provides up to 20 related occupations per source.

rules.name: is_primary consistent with relatedness_tier
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_transitions WHERE (relatedness_tier IN ('Primary-Short', 'Primary-Long') AND is_primary != true) OR (relatedness_tier = 'Supplemental' AND is_primary != false)
rules.description: is_primary must be true for Primary-Short and Primary-Long tiers, false for Supplemental. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: is_primary True=8,072 (50.6%), matching Primary-Short (4,134) + Primary-Long (3,938) = 8,072. False=7,872, matching Supplemental count. Spec defines is_primary from tier classification.

rules.name: Career transitions referential integrity to work profiles
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_transitions ct LEFT JOIN consumable.onet_work_profiles wp ON ct.bls_soc_code = wp.bls_soc_code WHERE wp.bls_soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: Every source bls_soc_code in career_transitions must exist in onet_work_profiles. Maps to ADV-FK-VALID and CONS-CROSS-TABLE patterns.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'All source and related SOCs exist in base.onet_occupations.' Both tables derive from the same 798-occupation base. Logical model shows CAREER_TRANSITION references WORK_PROFILE.

rules.name: Career transitions required fields not null
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_transitions WHERE record_id IS NULL OR bls_soc_code IS NULL OR related_bls_soc_code IS NULL OR source_title IS NULL OR related_title IS NULL OR best_index IS NULL OR relatedness_tier IS NULL OR is_primary IS NULL OR relationship_type IS NULL OR backs_feature IS NULL OR source_load_date IS NULL OR promoted_at IS NULL
rules.description: All fields in career_transitions are marked as required per spec. No nulls allowed.
rules.rationale: Spec marks every field in career_transitions as 'required: yes.' EDA confirms 0% null rate for all source fields. Derived fields (titles, flags) are set during transformation.

rules.name: SOC code format in career transitions: XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS violations FROM consumable.career_transitions WHERE bls_soc_code !~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$' OR related_bls_soc_code !~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$'
rules.description: Both bls_soc_code and related_bls_soc_code must match SOC format XX-XXXX.
rules.rationale: EDA report: all bls_soc_code (798 distinct) and related_bls_soc_code (796 distinct) values match XX-XXXX format in Silver source.

rules.name: Entity coverage: all 798 occupations in work profiles
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT bls_soc_code) = 798 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.onet_work_profiles
rules.description: All 798 occupations from base.onet_occupations must be present. Maps to ADV-ENTITY-COVERAGE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 798 occupations in source. 1:1 carry-forward, no filtering. Every occupation gets a row (774 with scores, 24 with nulls).

rules.name: Entity coverage: all 798 source SOCs in career transitions
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT bls_soc_code) = 798 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.career_transitions
rules.description: All 798 occupations must appear as source SOCs in career transitions. Maps to ADV-ENTITY-COVERAGE pattern.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'bls_soc_code (source): 798 distinct (all occupations have transitions, including partial-data ones).' Complete entity coverage in Silver source.

rules.name: Golden dataset verification
rules.sql: -- DEFERRED: Golden dataset not yet created. Rule SQL will be populated when governance/golden-datasets/gold-onet-profiles-golden.json exists. Must verify at least 3 independently computed values (e.g., Software Developers hmn_score, Registered Nurses burnout_score, a low-HMN occupation) match pipeline output within tolerance.
rules.description: CONS-GOLDEN-DATASET: At least 3 independently verifiable values must match pipeline output. Maps to CONS-GOLDEN-DATASET pattern. DEFERRED pending golden dataset creation by @primary-agent.
rules.rationale: Golden dataset file governance/golden-datasets/gold-onet-profiles-golden.json does not yet exist. Spec identifies 3 golden dataset candidates: Software Developers (15-1252), Registered Nurses (29-1141), and a low-HMN occupation. Rule will be activated once the golden dataset is created with hand-computed derivation chains.
rules.deferred_reason: Golden dataset not yet created. Requires @primary-agent implementation first.
